channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Risks of Aggressive Accounting: Lessons from Macy's
item.description: Aggressive accounting risks, internal controls, governance risk and compliance practices, and cybersecurity measures to prevent manipulation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Khanna says Democrats will work with Musk's DOGE on cutting defense budget
item.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) on Monday suggested that Democrats could work with tech billionaire Elon Musk’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) on cutting the defense budget. The issue came up during an interview Khanna gave to CNN anchor Jim Acosta, who noted the new efficiency panel.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft investigating outages affecting Outlook, Teams
item.description: An outage at Microsoft impacted thousands of online users on Monday as they attempted to use its Microsoft 365 service for emails, calendars and other features.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meet the USC student rocket club that shattered a world altitude record
item.description: This could not be happening. Five years after becoming the first students to launch a rocket into space, the University of Southern California Rocket Propulsion Lab (USCRPL) was readying a redesigned model, Aftershock II, for an even higher ascent from Nevada’s Black Rock Desert.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: These 4 fantastic sites offer more than 17,000 free online courses
item.description: The kids are back in school, the year is winding down, and maybe—just maybe—you’re finding yourself with a teeny, tiny bit of extra time on your hands.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Judge hears closing arguments on whether Google's advertising tech constitutes a monopoly
item.description: The Justice Department and Google have made their closing arguments in a trial alleging Google’s online advertising technology constitutes an illegal monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Barakat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Social media sites call for Australia to delay its ban on children younger than 16
item.description: An advocate for major social media platforms has told Australia's Parliament that a plan to ban children younger than 16 from the sites should be delayed rather than being rushed to approval this week.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: 5 ways AI should be fixing travel planning right now
item.description: Recently, I attempted to plan a road trip through Canada. What should have been exciting—imagining stunning national parks and scenic drives—became a digital nightmare of juggling 50 different websites.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smart, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk attacks Australian government over proposed social media ban
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister on Friday rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intended to control all Australians’ access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems
item.description: The massive convergence of cyber and physical systems has prompted the need for a framework based on engineering principles, the NIST fellow says. The post Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Angular 19 bolsters server-side rendering with incremental hydration
item.description: Angular 19, the latest version of Google’s TypeScript-based web development framework, has arrived. The update introduces incremental hydration and a route-level render mode, both in developer preview, and enables event replay by default. The release was officially unveiled on November 19.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Windows 11 tool can fix devices that won’t boot remotely
item.description: Microsoft is working on a new Windows feature, “Quick Machine Recovery,” that will allow IT administrators to use Windows Update with “targeted fixes” to remotely fix systems that can’t boot, according to Bleeping Computer.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SONiC pioneer Aviz Networks raises $17M for AI network management push
item.description: Networking software provider Aviz Networks today announced a $17 million Series A funding round to accelerate its growth in open networking solutions and artificial intelligence capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple admins: Update your hardware now
item.description: Among the first things Apple IT admins woke up to this morning was news of a pair of actively exploited zero-day attacks in the wild targeting Intel Macs, iPhones, iPads, and even Vision Pro users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Classes and coroutines
item.description: Java is one of the classic object-oriented languages, so classes and objects are of special interest to Java developers. Object-oriented programming gives you considerable power, particularly when it comes to structuring and organizing programs as they grow larger.
item.dc:creator: Info World
